title: NGE plot in three 25 hour movies
display_title: NGE plot in three 25 hour movies
wikitext:
[]: NGE plot in three 2.5 hour movies             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Fri Dec 13, 2013 3:37 pm Live Action Evangelion section is locked, so I guess I'll just post this fanfart here: (These are main plot-points, so I am missing smaller personal stuff that should be included, but this gives an idea of what I think should be cut and how it should be divided. Forgive me for Ersatz angel names. I'm lazy.) 1st Movie: Skull angel plot Shinji gets beaten up at school and runs away before moving in with Misato. Meets Kensuke camping, is brought back, decides he's going to leave. Misato persuades him to stay at train station. Moves in with Misato, gets simulation training in Eva. Meets Rei at her appartment. Hypercube angel plot Asuka comes to Japan (manga plot, she defeats Aquatic angel all by herself) Dancing angel plot 2nd Movie: Opens with Falling Sky angel operation in-the-field, Asuka is pissed that she couldn't defeat the angel herself. Her past and issues are hinted at. Shinji surpasses Asuka in the sychro test, she taunts him, Zebra Ball angel plot. Infected Toji plot (maybe manga version to give Toji and Shinji a personal moment?) Angel of Power plot (but Shinji doesn't get absorbed) Kaworu arrives, foreshadowing Asuka's replacement (manga plot) Bird Angel plot / Asuka mindrape. (Kaworu in Nerv, like in manga) Asuka in comma, Shinji flabbergasted, Halo angel detected: cliff-hanger (see manga) 3rd Movie: Kaworu and Rei vs Halo angel, Rei self-destructs (manga version) Rei clone reveal Kaji sends data to Misato, then gets killed for it Misato is depressed. Data exposition covers all the origin of Nerv stuff. Final Angel arc, Kaworu is put down by Shinji (manga version) Government moves in, Asuka is forced to fight them, discovers her mother's soul Shinji is escorted to the Eva by Misato, exposition on origin of humanity, angels, and vague idea of Seele's motivations. Asuka dies, Shinji comes too late, gets crucified. Kitchen scene, Instrumentality begins. Few scenes a-la TV instrumentality (to the point interpersonal, psychological, and philosophical stuff, or play it A Christmas Carol style, having 3 alternate universe scenes sequentially with Instrumentality hivemind being Shinji's guide: World where Shinji doesn't exist, world where only he exists, and perfect world.) Shinji ends Instrumentality Beach scene [This would probably be changed to something less jarring and ambiguous in a Hollywood-size movie, even in a faithful adaption. Heck, Sadamoto gave his own positive twist, and it was okay.]

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Fri Dec 13, 2013 5:55 pm I don't really see a point in cutting down or rather reduce the complex and multifarious plot of Evangelion, especially because ~11,45 hours (~10 hours if you exclude out the rather trivial and kind of awkward episodes (7 and 10)) aren't much more than ~7,5. Although I don't doubt that there are scenes and plot point that aren't necessary to tell the story or that some scenes were better implemented in the manga. The biggest problem I have with this idea, is that NGE works really just fine as a show (e.g. "monster of the week"-kind of plot-telling) and especially as a "old show". I'd declare people who wouldn't watch Evangelion because it looks "too old" as not capable or keen of watching EVA. A little issue I have with your choices of Angels: *Zeruel: You're forgetting the really important S^2- / Berserk-plot *Iruel = Important because of the implementation of the magi *Leliel = Why are you leaving out the absorbation ? THis was freaking important to Shinji's mental development. Although I like the idea of having the whole show compressed into a handy series of movies (This was what I expected the Rebuilds to be ) but I doubt that it's possible without leaving out important parts. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 13, 2013 8:07 pm Gorbatschow wrote: Leliel = Why are you leaving out the absorbation ? THis was freaking important to Shinji's mental development.

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Fri Dec 13, 2013 9:13 pm Gorbatschow wrote: A little issue I have with your choices of Angels: *Zeruel: You're forgetting the really important S^2- / Berserk-plot *Iruel = Important because of the implementation of the magi *Leliel = Why are you leaving out the absorbation ? THis was freaking important to Shinji's mental development.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 13, 2013 11:07 pm Angel of Will wrote: Zeruel is the Angel of Power

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Sat Dec 14, 2013 2:14 am I think 20 is pretty different from the episodes around it. I like 20, but I am trying to fit a lot of stuff in, and there are other ways in on the mother-in-the-Eva, and Shinji-wants-to-escape-the-world angles. Heck, maybe we can turn the Zebra Ball into a symbiotic organism that keeps Eva-01 and Shinji alive, and Shinji can try using that to hide in a hole with his mommy.

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Wed Dec 18, 2013 1:36 pm Angel of Will wrote: I think 20 is pretty different from the episodes around it. I like 20, but I am trying to fit a lot of stuff in, and there are other ways in on the mother-in-the-Eva, and Shinji-wants-to-escape-the-world angles. Heck, maybe we can turn the Zebra Ball into a symbiotic organism that keeps Eva-01 and Shinji alive, and Shinji can try using that to hide in a hole with his mommy.

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Wed Dec 18, 2013 2:26 pm I had always looked at that NTE moment as Shinji going berserk instead of his mother, which makes it an important moment in the franchise, as the character is going beyond his usual problems (only to run into new ones in 3.0). Also, that interpretation would jive with the whole 'placing the choker on a person who risks divine awakening or whatever it was called.'

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Wed Dec 18, 2013 2:37 pm Angel of Will wrote: I had always looked at that NTE moment as Shinji going berserk instead of his mother, which makes it an important moment in the franchise, as the character is going beyond his usual problems (only to run into new ones in 3.0). Also, that interpretation would jive with the whole 'placing the choker on a person who risks divine awakening or whatever it was called.'

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 18, 2013 3:00 pm SoryuUberAlles wrote: A10 headset

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Wed Dec 18, 2013 3:05 pm Reichu wrote: Interface headset. :p

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 18, 2013 3:27 pm SoryuUberAlles wrote: And for the past 30 minutes, I thought OAV stood for 'on-air version' as opposed to DC. Must wiki harder.

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Wed Dec 18, 2013 4:37 pm Halo: CE: CE

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Wed Dec 18, 2013 5:09 pm Reichu wrote: This makes the assertion in my previous post incorrect, but also highlights why it would be a terrible idea to acronymize the On-Air Versions to OAV.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Dec 18, 2013 6:31 pm Reichu wrote: it would be a terrible idea to acronymize the On-Air Versions to OAV.

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Wed Dec 18, 2013 7:12 pm SoryuUberAlles wrote: So where does Shinji go after de-skulling Skull Angel? Is he still in the hospital?

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Wed Dec 18, 2013 8:02 pm Angel of Will wrote: I was thinking Shinji would live alone in the apartment/room that was assigned to him until Misato took him in later.

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Wed Dec 18, 2013 8:52 pm Heheh, no. This is just a fanfart of mine. My serious Eva-related project is a sequel doujin, which has its own thread. But it is good for moviemakers to have available reference for what fans think would make a good production, whether they would listen or not. I did hear that the LOTR movies had community input. I disagree with the 'cutting out exposition' point. Refer back to my last comment about how much time has been cut out. The major exposition is in the episodes and manga arcs described on my list. Edit: In addition, the NTE movies aren't as long as 2.5 hours each; the third movie was just an hour and a half, I think.

[]:             Quote Post by LegionWrex » Fri Dec 27, 2013 10:23 pm As a aspiring screenwriter, I find that it is my duty to nitpick every story idea ever and basically make it worthless. But seriously, let's truly look at this. 1st Movie (I'll name this movie Evangelion: Rise of Angels or just Evangelion): Skull Angel Plot - His name is Sachiel BTW Anyways, keep this in it's entirety. It is important development for the plot, Shinji's character, and it sets up all major plot points. Shinji gets beaten up/Runs Away/Moves in with Misato - It seems you are missing the Shamshel plot. Either A) You stuck it in with this or B) Cut it out. If it is A), then good. Keeping this in is important. It will give Toji and Kensuke the little character development they do have, and give Shinji a moment of badassery (which is always nice). I would cut the run away part, as it will happen again later on so it would be redundant. Moving in with Misato should stay in. Kensuke camping/Leaves/Stays - Keep this is in for obvious character reasons. Simulation training - Keep in, possible cut from final film (it may end up feeling pointless in the long run). Rei's apartment - Keep in again for obvious reasons. Ramiel plot - This more then likely will be the big action scene of the film, and the big epic thing all the trailers would show off. So, of course, keep it in. Asuka reveal - Agreed, I would the manga version. It would show just how talented Asuka is. Israfil plot - Good choice for a climax. We edit it a bit to slightly wrap up some of the film's plotlines, but also leave them hanging for the sequel (at this point we aren't sure if it will get a sequel or not). I would end the film on something big, however, like maybe the reveal of Lilith or something like that. That would get people pumped up for part 2. 2nd movie (I would title this movie Evangelion: The Age of Death, Evangelion: The Impending Doom or simply Evangelion 2. Evangelion: Part 2 would be another good title.): Sahaquiel plot - Before we get to this, I would open up the film with Second Impact. Naturally, the 2nd film will be darker, so let the audience know as the movie begins that this will be darker. Also, thanks for cutting Sandalphon and Matarael. They would add nothing to the final film. Now, back to the Sahaquiel plot. Opening it up in the middle of the fight is a smart move writing wise. Leliel plot - I would keep this in solely due to the fact that it's my favourite episode, but... yeah. Keeping it in would make a lot of sense. Bardiel plot - Manga version all the way. Toji dying would give greater emotional impact. Zeruel plot - I would have Shinji get absorbed, but only because major things happen during his "imprisonment" within the Eva. Kaworu arrives - Great idea using the manga version. It would give more connection to Kaworu and Shinji. Arael plot - Same as yours. Cliffhanger ending - More then likely then the final 2 films would be shot back-to-back, so a cliffhanger would make sense, even if it would a cop-out. 3rd movie (I would title this movie Evangelion: Finale, Evangelion 3 or Evangelion Part 3.): Armisael plot - Once again, same as yours. Rei clone reveal - Same. Kaji dies - Honestly, I would keep whether Kaji died ambiguous, but still imply that he is dead. Tabris plot - Same. Asuka fights the military - Same. Exposition on angel origins - Instead of it just being dialogue (which is incredibly boring), I would visuals to help tell the exposition. Asuka dies - Nope. I would use the manga version were Asuka survives and Shinji mans up. Greater emotional impact. Instrumentality - This is where we should tread lightly. Casual audiences and film studios would not respond well these scenes, since they are philosophical, metaphorical and confusing. But for the most, I would keep it all in and hope to god it doesn't get an NC-17 rating. TV instrumentality - I would use both the EoE and TV for this one. Mix them in together in such a way so that it makes sense. End of the movie (Instrumentality over/Beach scene) - Yeah, it would probably be changed, but personally, I hated the last chapter of the manga because of that. Personally, felt that ambiguity actually felt conclusive. I don't think it needs any changing. This, of course is just my opinion. Because of that, it's probably really stupid But yeah. That's is how I would change or "improve" your idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Angel of Will » Sat Dec 28, 2013 12:47 am I really felt that in order to include Asuka in the first movie, I had to cut the red bug angel. It's why I was confident in including Kensuke's camping scene in the run-away arc, and keeping Toji's development around the fourth child arc. As for Instrumentality, I made a personal edit collecting the tv ending, the movie, and the deleted scene that makes thematic sense. My idea was to distill some main concepts and make it more grounded- have the language and allegories be clearer (I made a Christmas Carol reference that relates an already understood narrative technique from being a part of pop culture). That way, even though it's philosophical, it literally has a character (the hivemind) holding the viewer by the hand, as much as the ghosts of Christmas was holding Scrooge by the hand. I mean, it shouldn't be dumbed down in any way. That would lose the coolness factor of having an intensely off-the-wall climax. But it should be clarified- as much as a philosopher speaking to a public audience without the academic jargon. (I consider the 2nd and 3rd Matrix movies being sensible from a philosophy student's point of view, but not from any kind of casual movie-goer's, and I think there's a conversation to be had about the role of a director to make a huge budget movie like that accessible, albeit intelligent.) Oh yeah! The Shinji saving Asuka bit. I found this to be really cool in the manga. It was the manga moment version of Shinji getting balls at the end of 2.22. But it would render the kitchen scene and beach scene impossible. Asuka needs to die with a grudge against Shinji for her to be cold enough to reject his cry for help, pushing him off the edge to start Instrumentality. I know the manga got him to it with a more general reason, but the movie version's kitchen scene is just so personally focused and powerful because of it.

Forgive me for Ersatz angel names. I'm lazy.) 1st Movie: Sku

That way, even though it's philosophical, it literally has a character (the hivemind) holding the viewer by the hand, as much as the ghosts of Christmas was holding Scrooge by the hand. I mean, it shouldn't be dumbed down in any way. That would lose the coolness factor of having an intensely off-the-wall climax. But it should be clarified- as much as a philosopher speaking to a public audience without the academic jargon. (I consider the 2nd and 3rd Matrix movies being sensible from a philosophy student's point of view, but not from any kind of casual movie-goer's, and I think there's a conversation to be had about the role of a director to make a huge budget movie like that accessible, albeit intelligent.) Oh yeah! The Shinji saving Asuka bit. I found this to be really cool in the manga. It was the manga moment version of Shinji getting balls at the end of 2.22. But it would render the kitchen scene and beach scene impossible. Asuka needs to die with a grudge against Shinji for her to be cold enough to reject his cry for help, pushing him off the edge to start Instrumentality. I know the manga got him to it with a more general reason, but the movie version's kitchen scene is just so personally focused and powerful because of it.
